title: Managing time with tense and aspect
description: Using what you've already learned about tense and aspect, use your time wizardry to make sense of these sentences.
topic_path: Managing time with tense and aspect

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Yesterday, I \_\_\_\_\_\_ breakfast at 6:30.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that happened "yesterday", so we should use a **past tense** verb.
Of the choices, only **"ate"** is a past tense verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Next summer, Erin \_\_\_\_\_\_ monkeys in the forests of West Africa.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that will happen "next summer", so we should use the *future tense*.
Of the choices, only **"will study"** is a future tense verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Courtney \_\_\_\_\_\_ to Rochester before.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that has never happened until the present ("before"), so we should use the *present perfect tense*.
Of the choices, only **"has never been"** is a present perfect tense verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Mark \_\_\_\_\_\_ when the doorbell rang.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that was ongoing in the *past*, so we should use the *past progressive tense*.
Of the choices, only **"was sleeping"** is a past progressive verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Simon \_\_\_\_\_\_ the crime scene carefully before he began taking pictures.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that happened in the *past*, so we should use the *past tense*.
Of the choices, only **"examined"** is a past tense verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Unfortunately, we are \_\_\_\_\_\_ from going in the Cave of Flames.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence uses "prohibit" as an *adjective*, so we should use the *past participle* form.
Of the choices, only **"prohibited"** is a past participle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>We had been \_\_\_\_\_\_ that there would be no bats in here!  I’m allergic to bats!

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence uses "had been" as to set up the blank, so we should use the *past participle* form of "assure".
Of the choices, only **"assured"** is a past participle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Mayra was so \_\_\_\_\_\_ in her work that she didn’t notice that the lights had gone off.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence uses "engage" as an *adjective*, so we should use the *past participle* form.
Of the choices, only **"engaged"** is a past participle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Wanda \_\_\_\_\_\_ a technical diagram in her studio when the clock struck midnight.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that was ongoing in the *past*, so we should use the *past progressive tense*.
Of the choices, only **"was illustrating"** is a past progressive verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Jeremy is \_\_\_\_\_\_ me to apply for the job.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that is ongoing in the *present*, so we should use the *progressive tense*.
Of the choices, only **"urging"** creates a progressive verb alongside "is".

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>In the future, I hope we \_\_\_\_\_\_ with aliens!

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that will happen "in the future", so we should use the *future tense*.
Of the choices, only **"will trade"** is a future tense verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>James \_\_\_\_\_\_ against his parents when he was a kid by wearing mismatched socks.  They didn’t notice.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that happened in the *past* ("when he was a kid"), so we should use the *past tense*.
Of the choices, only **"rebelled"** is a past tense verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Dr. Kramer will \_\_\_\_\_\_ the X-ray and project it on that screen in front of you.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that will happen in the *future*, so we should use the *future tense*.
Of the choices, only **"magnify"** creates a future tense verb alongside "will".

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Danielle \_\_\_\_\_\_ the vase carefully, but she couldn’t find any fractures.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that happened in the *past*, so we should use the *past tense*.
Of the choices, only **"inspected"** is a past tense verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>In a few days from now, the mold \_\_\_\_\_\_ the entire corridor.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that will be completed in the *future* ("a few days from now"), so we should use the *future perfect tense*.
Of the choices, only **"will have infected"** is a future perfect verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Each morning, Fred \_\_\_\_\_\_ the tarp from his car.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that happens every day, so we should use the *present tense*.
Of the choices, only **"removes"** is a present tense verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>The rope is \_\_\_\_\_\_ too quickly!  You’re going to have to jump!

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that is ongoing in the *present*, so we should use the *progressive tense*.
Of the choices, only **"fraying"** creates a progressive verb alongside "is".

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the form of the verb that best matches the time frame of the sentence.**

>Ira \_\_\_\_\_\_ with lightning speed and caught the kitten as it fell from the tree.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This sentence describes something that happened in the *past*, so we should use the *past tense*.
Of the choices, only **"reacted"** is a past tense verb, so it's the best verb to use in this sentence.
